question: Design a `Time` class with private `hour`, `minute`, and `second` integer members. Provide public setter methods (`setHour`, `setMinute`, `setSecond`) that validate the input to ensure it falls within valid ranges (e.g., hour 0-23).

test_cases.input: Time myTime; myTime.setHour(15); myTime.setMinute(30); myTime.setSecond(45); myTime.setHour(25); myTime.setMinute(-1); myTime.setSecond(60);
test_cases.expected:
Time object created, initialized to 00:00:00.
Displaying current time: 00:00:00

--- Testing valid values ---
--- Attempting to set hour to 15 ---
Hour 15 is not negative. Good so far.
Hour 15 is not too high. Valid range.
Successfully updated hour to 15.
Current hour in object: 15
--- Attempting to set minute to 30 ---
Minute 30 is not negative. Good so far.
Minute 30 is not too high. Valid range.
Successfully updated minute to 30.
Current minute in object: 30
--- Attempting to set second to 45 ---
Second 45 is not negative. Good so far.
Second 45 is not too high. Valid range.
Successfully updated second to 45.
Current second in object: 45
Displaying current time: 15:30:45

--- Testing invalid values ---
--- Attempting to set hour to 25 ---
Hour 25 is not negative. Good so far.
Hour 25 is greater than the maximum allowed (23). Hour not set.
Current hour in object: 15
--- Attempting to set minute to -1 ---
Minute -1 is less than the minimum allowed (0). Minute not set.
Current minute in object: 30
--- Attempting to set second to 60 ---
Second 60 is not negative. Good so far.
Second 60 is greater than the maximum allowed (59). Second not set.
Current second in object: 45
Displaying current time: 15:30:45

--- Testing edge cases ---
--- Attempting to set hour to 0 ---
Hour 0 is not negative. Good so far.
Hour 0 is not too high. Valid range.
Successfully updated hour to 0.
Current hour in object: 0
--- Attempting to set minute to 59 ---
Minute 59 is not negative. Good so far.
Minute 59 is not too high. Valid range.
Successfully updated minute to 59.
Current minute in object: 59
--- Attempting to set second to 0 ---
Second 0 is not negative. Good so far.
Second 0 is not too high. Valid range.
Successfully updated second to 0.
Current second in object: 0
Displaying current time: 00:59:00

errors: Setter methods contain excessive `std::cout` statements for validation and status updates, leading to noisy output and reduced efficiency for simple operations, violating the principle of 'least surprise' for method side effects.

Validation logic uses deeply nested `if` statements (e.g., `if (cond1) { if (cond2) { ... } else { ... } } else { ... }`) which are less readable and more complex than combined logical operators (e.g., `if (cond1 && cond2)`).

Magic numbers (0, 23, 59) are used directly throughout the validation logic instead of named constants, impacting readability and making future modifications (e.g., changing time format) more error-prone and less maintainable.
The repetitive code structure across the setter methods (similar validation checks and output messages) indicates a lack of abstraction and could be refactored for better efficiency and readability.
